candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above EBIT, but it is not labelled as cost of revenues / cost of sales and appears to sit in the broader operating expense section rather than as a COGS subline. The selected COGS bucket is only 'Cost of Revenues - Total'.

Looks like vessel-related depreciation, which is more naturally tied to vessel operating assets than to the selected COGS row 'Cost of Revenues - Total'. It appears as a component of total depreciation, not as a COGS-labelled D&A row.
Component of total depreciation. Not explicitly tied to cost of revenues, and likely operating lease asset depreciation rather than a COGS-specific D&A line.
Balancing/minor line, likely part of depreciation detail rather than a distinct COGS D&A row to add separately.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortisation row is labelled as belonging to cost of revenues / cost of sales. The visible depreciation rows appear to be separate operating depreciation, mainly vessel-related, rather than a COGS-specific D&A line to add to the selected COGS bucket.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above EBIT and outside the selected SG&A rows, but it is not specifically labelled as administrative / selling / R&D depreciation. The detailed component 'Depreciation vessels' suggests the total is dominated by vessel depreciation rather than SG&A D&A.
Explicitly vessel-related depreciation, so it does not fit the selected SG&A bucket of commercial/admin/salaries/other admin expenses.
A depreciation component, but not clearly tied to SG&A. No wording links it to admin, selling, or R&D, and it sits under total depreciation detail.
Appears to be a balancing/detail line within depreciation, not a distinct SG&A D&A row.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket consists of commercial/admin services, salaries, and other operating/admin expenses. Although depreciation is shown separately above EBIT, the detail line 'Depreciation vessels' indicates the depreciation block is primarily vessel-related rather than SG&A. No explicit SG&A-, admin-, or R&D-specific D&A row is visible, so no separate XSGA_DA row should be added.
